name: Vegetarian Chili
recipeInstructions:
1. Transfer the kidney beans and the soaking water to a large heavy saucepan. Add 1 teaspoon of salt and bring to a boil over high heat. Lower the heat and continue boiling the beans, partially covered, until tender (about 1 hour). Add more water as necessary to prevent scorching.
2. Meanwhile, place tomato juice in another saucepan and bring to a boil over medium heat. Remove from heat immediately and add bulghur to the juice. Cover and let stand for 15 minutes. It should be slightly crunchy. Set aside.
3. Heat olive oil in a large heavy pot over medium heat. Add onions and garlic and cook until translucent. Add celery, carrots, tomatoes, lemon juice, and all spices (including remaining salt) to onions and cook, covered, until vegetables are nearly tender (about 10-15 minutes). Add bell peppers and continue cooking another 10 minutes.
4. Add kidney beans, soaking water, and bulghur to the vegetables in the pot. Stir thoroughly and simmer for 30 minutes over low heat. Adjust seasoning as needed. If thick, add water occasionally and stir to prevent sticking.
